mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC consistently falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST yields a negative or flat 20-day forward return distribution, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 2.8% annual threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.8%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC consistently falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand, which validates earnings upgrade cycles and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when UNRATE simultaneously rises above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising retail sales indicates inventory depletion and supply chain bottlenecks, which triggers short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.
